task_yaml.task:
I've been watching YouTube videos about Python virtualenv management on
macOS Sonoma and I'm more confused than when I started — every video
contradicts the last. I just need one clear answer. Use the
YouTube transcript skill to read through the five transcripts under
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/transcripts/ and synthesize what the
actual best current workflow is, rather than summarizing each video in
isolation. They were uploaded between 2022 and 2024 and they disagree.


Here's the mess: the 2022 video says just use python -m venv. A 2023
video says that advice is outdated and recommends pyenv plus Poetry.
Then a different 2023 video warns that pyenv plus Poetry has a known
compatibility issue on macOS Sonoma specifically. A 2024 video claims a
newer tool fixes everything. The fifth is more of a history lesson but
might still be useful for context.

I'm on Sonoma right now. I want the single recommended workflow I
should adopt today, with a clear note on which earlier recommendations
are now superseded and why, plus any caveats. Commit to one answer —
don't hedge with five.

Write the synthesis to /tmp_workspace/results/answer.md. Make it
concrete: name the specific tool I should install, walk through install
plus first-project setup, and for each older recommendation explain in
one or two sentences why it is no longer the best choice for me. If a
recommendation is outdated for a specific reason — like a macOS Sonoma
compatibility bug — call that out by name rather than vaguely saying
"it's older."

Cite the videos you draw from. Use either the video title or the
video_id from each transcript file so I can trace your reasoning back
to a specific source.

At the bottom of answer.md include a short "Caveats" section noting
any limitations of the recommended tool that came up in the videos —
even videos that overall recommended it. I don't want only the upside;
if a video flagged a real limitation, surface it.
